headline: Italian court rules Netflix price-hike clauses are void, orders refunds
summary: A Rome court has ruled as unlawful price increases imposed by Netflix on its Italian subscribers over the last seven years, and ‌has ordered the U.S. media giant to reimburse them, a consumer association said ‌on Friday.  Consumer group Movimento Consumatori said in a statement that the court had upheld its lawsuit against Netflix Italia ​and deemed unfair clauses that allowed subscription price increases from 2017 to January 2024.  Netflix said in a statement that it would appeal against the court's decision.

headline: Derating and Acquisition Concerns Pressured Netflix (NFLX) in Q4
summary: Magellan Asset Management, an investment management company, released the fourth quarter 2025 investor letter for “Magellan Global Fund”. A copy of the letter can be downloaded here. headline: Price Hikes Driving U.S. Streaming Video Sales Growth, Not New Subscribers
summary: Netflix and other subscription streaming video services are turning to price increases and advertising to drive revenue growth.

raw.headline: Price Hikes Driving U.S. Streaming Video Sales Growth, Not New Subscribers

headline: Netflix (NFLX) Valuation Check After Recent Share Price Momentum Fuels Mixed Undervaluation Signals
summary: Netflix (NFLX) remains in focus for investors after recent share price moves, with the stock showing a 3.3% one-day return and 5.7% over the past week, keeping attention on its current valuation. See our latest analysis for Netflix. Looking beyond the latest move, Netflix’s 8.4% year to date share price return and 7.6% one year total shareholder return sit alongside a very large 3 year total shareholder return near 3x, suggesting momentum has been building over a longer horizon.
